runtime.boundary: pinned Python reference runner on Apple Silicon; not an accepted product dependency or Rust boundary

options.llama_cpp_assumption: none; current official vLLM realtime support was recorded instead

energy.notes: No accepted Apple-local runtime reached inference.

engineering_cost.conversion_effort: The pinned 4-bit MLX conversion proves the causal encoder, adapter, delay-conditioned decoder, tokenizer, and weights on Apple Silicon. Product work remains around an accepted repository-owned or narrower external boundary.
engineering_cost.update_cost: Track the official Transformers architecture plus the pinned MLX oracle; decide between a direct official-MLX adapter and the substantially smaller pure-C/MPS boundary only after quality and streaming lifecycle gates.
engineering_cost.packaging_cost: The measured 4-bit snapshot is 3.15 GB and peaked near 5.8 GB of MLX allocation. The official BF16 repository remains about 17.77 GB and the recommended vLLM path requires at least 16 GB GPU memory.

result.blocker: Apple-local reference quality is now measured, but live input streaming, cancellation, lifecycle behavior, and an accepted production boundary are not. The Python MLX oracle cannot be promoted directly under ADR-0005.
observations: The old vLLM/CUDA-only disposition was stale: official Transformers support and publisher-linked ExecuTorch, C/MPS, MLX, and Rust paths now exist.
At 480 ms, the 15-clip audiobook pilot reached 5.84% macro WER and 2.72% German WER. It beat the existing Whisper result in English and Portuguese while trailing it in Spanish and French.
At 2400 ms, macro WER improved to 4.25%, German to 0.68%, Spanish to 5.31%, and French to 1.74%; English and Portuguese regressed. Delay therefore belongs in the language-specific selection contract.
The 2400 ms development result is slightly below the existing Whisper macro WER of 4.49%, but the pilot is too small and already development-exposed for product selection.
The quality runner consumes complete clips. It does not yet prove live-input first-result latency despite the model and reference implementation exposing stateful streaming.
